# FEAT056 — Wire chat.tsx to v4 dispatcher + general_assistant skill

> **Note on numbering:** The dev plan (`docs/v4/09_dev_plan.md §3`) referred to
> the v2.02 skill-batch work as FEAT080. To keep specs reviewable, that batch
> is being **split**: this FEAT056 covers chat.tsx wiring + the
> `general_assistant` skill only. Per-intent migrations
> (`task_management`, `notes`, `calendar`, `inbox_triage`,
> `emotional_checkin`) will be FEAT057+ — one PM spec each. Track by title,
> not number.

**Type:** feature
**Status:** Approved by user 2026-04-27 — stages 3–7 ran. Stage 2 review notes: split decision approved; per-intent migrations move to FEAT057+; no specific Q answered, deferred to architect.
**MoSCoW:** MUST
**Category:** Architecture
**Priority:** 1
**Release:** v2.02 (Phase 2 — first deliverable; unlocks the rest of v2.02)
**Tags:** skill-migration, chat-wiring, general-assistant, dual-path
**Created:** 2026-04-27

**Depends on:** FEAT054 (Done), FEAT051 (Done), FEAT055 (Done — provides `dispatchSkill`)
**Unblocks:** every subsequent skill migration (because the consumer wiring exists), FEAT083 Topics surface (needs the v4 path live in chat)

---

## Status

Draft — awaiting human review before architect picks it up for stages 3–4.

---

## Problem Statement

Phase 1 (FEAT054 + FEAT051 + FEAT055) built the entire v4 skill stack:
loader, orchestrator, dispatcher, and one POC skill (`priority_planning`). All
of it is in production code, validated by 89 tests, and **unreachable from the
chat surface.** The user asks "what should I focus on?" and gets the same
legacy `full_planning` response as before — the v4 path is dormant because
`chat.tsx:424` still calls `classifyIntentWithFallback`.

This feature wires the consumer. After this ships:
- Phrases that match a v4-enabled skill route through the orchestrator + dispatcher
- The `priority_planning` skill from FEAT055 actually handles user phrases
- The `general_assistant` skill provides a clean fallback (closes the FEAT051
  warning that fires every time fallback triggers without it)
- The dual-path window opens — both v4 and legacy run side-by-side, gated by
  `setV4SkillsEnabled([...])`. Per-intent migrations (FEAT057+) flip skills
  one at a time by adding ids to the enabled list.

This is the smallest scope that makes any of the v4 work user-visible. Without
it, every later v2.02 FEAT is also dormant.

---

## Goals

1. `chat.tsx` calls the v4 path **before** the legacy path. When a routed
   skill is in `getV4SkillsEnabled()`, the dispatcher handles the phrase end
   to end and the legacy code is skipped for that turn.
2. When v4 returns null (skill not enabled, or skill missing from registry),
   the chat surface falls through to legacy without any user-visible flicker.
3. A real `general_assistant` skill exists in `src/skills/` — handles
   freeform phrases that don't match any specialized skill. Closes the
   "fallback skill missing" warning from FEAT051.
4. The skill badge from FEAT051 Story 5 actually renders under v4-handled
   replies (the data was already there; chat.tsx just needs to read it).
5. Asking *"what should I focus on?"* now goes through the FEAT055
   `priority_planning` skill end-to-end (the POC becomes a working feature).
6. The headless runner is unaffected — it doesn't process user phrases for
   v2.02.

---

## Success Metrics

- A "what should I focus on?" phrase produces a chat reply rendered from the
  `priority_planning` skill's `submit_priority_ranking` handler output
  (verifiable via routing log + dispatch log entries with the same hashed
  phrase).
- A freeform phrase (e.g., "tell me a joke") routes to `general_assistant`
  and produces a sensible response.
- Setting `setV4SkillsEnabled([])` reverts ALL phrases to the legacy path —
  same UX as before v2.01.
- Setting `setV4SkillsEnabled(["priority_planning"])` (current boot config)
  routes only matching phrases to v4; everything else stays on legacy.
- All 265 existing tests still pass. New tests added for the wiring +
  general_assistant skill.
- `npm run build:web` exports successfully.

---

## User Stories

### Story 1 — `priority_planning` actually handles user phrases

**As a** user, **when** I type *"what should I focus on today?"* in chat,
**I want** the assistant to give me a focused ranking, **so that** the v4
priority_planning skill becomes a real working feature instead of dormant
infrastructure.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given the boot has called `setV4SkillsEnabled(["priority_planning",
      "general_assistant"])`, when I send *"what should I focus on today?"*,
      then the chat reply is generated by the `priority_planning` skill's
      handler (verifiable via the `[skillDispatcher] dispatch ...`
      structured log entry naming the skill and tool).
- [ ] The chat reply renders without UX regression — same bubble style,
      same loading state, same error handling as today.
- [ ] The legacy `priority_ranking`-related code paths in `llm.ts` and
      `assembler.ts` (if any) are NOT invoked for this phrase.

### Story 2 — `general_assistant` handles freeform

**As a** user, **when** I send a phrase that doesn't match any specialized
skill (e.g., *"tell me a joke"* or *"what do you think?"*), **I want** a
sensible response, **so that** the assistant doesn't fail on phrases the
specialized skills weren't designed for.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A `general_assistant` skill exists at `src/skills/general_assistant/`
      with all four required files. Its `triggerPhrases` are deliberately
      generic (covers freeform chat).
- [ ] Given `general_assistant` is enabled, when I send *"tell me about
      yourself"*, the orchestrator routes to `general_assistant` (either via
      embedding match if it scored highest, or via fallback) and the
      dispatcher returns a chat-style reply.
- [ ] The skill's prompt explicitly disclaims that it's freeform — it does
      NOT pretend to be a specialized skill. (No fake task creation, no fake
      calendar lookups; if the user asks something a real skill should
      handle, the assistant says so.)
- [ ] The FEAT051 fallback warning *"fallback skill missing"* no longer
      fires (`general_assistant` is now in the registry).

### Story 3 — Dual-path coexistence (rollback safety)

**As a** developer, **I want** to flip the v4 path off without code changes,
**so that** if a v4 skill produces bad output I can disable it instantly.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given `setV4SkillsEnabled([])` is the boot configuration, when any
      phrase arrives, the chat dispatch goes through the legacy
      `classifyIntentWithFallback` path. The v4 dispatcher is never called.
- [ ] Given `setV4SkillsEnabled(["priority_planning"])` is the boot
      configuration, when a "what should I focus on" phrase arrives, it
      goes through v4. When an "add a task" phrase arrives, it goes through
      legacy (because `task_management` is not yet in the enabled list).
- [ ] Removing all skill ids from the boot config (`setV4SkillsEnabled([])`)
      and restarting reverts the user-visible behavior 100% to pre-v2.02.
      No half-state, no orphaned UI.

### Story 4 — Skill badge rendering

**As a** user, **I want** to see which skill answered my message **so that**
I can correct misrouting and understand why a particular response was given.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] When a v4 skill handles a phrase, the chat bubble shows a small badge
      with the skill name (e.g., "priority_planning").
- [ ] When the legacy path handles a phrase, no badge is shown (preserves
      pre-v2.02 UX for non-migrated phrases).
- [ ] Tapping the badge shows: skill name, confidence, routing method
      (embedding / haiku / fallback / structural / direct), and a *"this
      didn't help"* action that logs a feedback event (full feedback
      pipeline ships with FEAT066 in Phase 6 — for now the action only
      writes a structured log entry).

### Story 5 — Graceful failure of v4 path

**As a** user, **I want** the assistant to keep working even if the v4
dispatcher fails, **so that** v4 bugs don't break my chat.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given the dispatcher returns a degraded result (`degraded.reason` set
      — e.g., LLM throw, unknown tool), the chat shows the dispatcher's
      degraded user message AND falls through to the legacy path so the
      user gets a real reply.

      *(Architect to decide: simpler alternative — if degraded, immediately
      fall through to legacy without showing the degraded message. UX call.)*
- [ ] Given the dispatcher throws (programmer error — should never happen
      per design review §6), the chat catches the exception, logs it, and
      falls through to legacy. The user never sees a stack trace.
- [ ] Given `dispatchSkill` returns `null` (skill not in enabled set, or
      skill missing from registry), the chat falls through to legacy
      silently — same flow as today.

### Story 6 — No regression for non-migrated phrases

**As a** user, **I want** my existing workflows to keep working unchanged.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Phrases that route to non-enabled intents (`task_create`,
      `calendar_query`, `emotional_checkin`, `full_planning`, etc.) produce
      the same response as before v2.02 — they go through the legacy path.
- [ ] All 265 existing tests still pass.
- [ ] `npm run build:web` exports successfully.

---

## Out of Scope

- **Migrating any specialized intent to a v4 skill** — that's FEAT057+
  (per-intent specs).
- **The Topics skill** — that's FEAT083 (separate spec, can ship in
  parallel).
- **Settings panel for `setV4SkillsEnabled`** — boot wiring stays in
  `app/_layout.tsx` for v2.02. FEAT035 settings panel ships in Phase 3.
- **Headless runner v4 routing** — the runner runs background jobs, not
  user phrases.
- **`priority_log` persistence** — the existing FEAT055 handler writes only
  to console. Persistence wiring deferred until the executor change pattern
  is proven by a real migration (FEAT057 task_management likely first).
- **Audit log table** — FEAT056 audit log ships in Phase 3.
- **Per-skill model fallback** — Sonnet skills that fail get the dispatcher's
  degraded result; no automatic Haiku fallback.

---

## Assumptions & Open Questions

**Assumptions:**
- The dispatch point in `app/(tabs)/chat.tsx` around line 424 (where
  `classifyIntentWithFallback` is called today) is the correct insertion
  point for the v4 hook.
- The chat surface's existing render path can handle the dispatcher's
  `userMessage` string the same way it handles legacy responses (one
  bubble per response).
- The badge UI is a small additive change — no existing chat layout
  refactor required.
- `general_assistant` can be a Haiku-tier skill (cheap, freeform). Not
  Sonnet — there's no specialized reasoning required.

**Open Questions for the Architect:**
1. Where exactly in chat.tsx does the v4 hook insert? Before
   `classifyIntentWithFallback` (line 424)? Or before the triage step
   that runs at the top of the handler? Architect call after reading
   the surrounding code.
2. Story 5 ambiguity: when dispatcher returns a degraded result, should
   chat (a) show the degraded message AND fall through to legacy, or (b)
   silently fall through to legacy (user never sees the degraded message)?
   PM proposes (b) — degraded means "v4 couldn't handle it, try legacy
   like nothing happened" — cleaner UX. Architect decides.
3. The existing chat surface has multi-turn pending-context handling
   (`s._pendingContext`, `_pendingTriage`). How does v4 integrate with
   that? Two options:
   - (a) v4 also supports pending-context → would require the dispatcher
     to be aware of multi-turn state.
   - (b) v4 skills do NOT support pending-context for v2.02 → if a phrase
     arrives during a pending state, it bypasses v4 and goes to legacy.
     Simpler. PM proposes (b).
4. Skill badge UX — small text under the bubble vs. a tag-style chip vs.
   a colored dot? Architect / UX call.
5. Where does the badge tap action live? An `Alert.alert()` popup is
   simplest; a custom modal is nicer. PM proposes Alert for v2.02.
6. `general_assistant` token budget — 3000? 5000? It's freeform, so on
   the higher end probably. Architect to decide.
7. Should `general_assistant` declare any specific data category in
   `dataSchemas.read`? PM proposes minimum: `["objectives", "userProfile"]`
   so the assistant has basic context but isn't reading sensitive data.

---

## Architecture Notes

*Filled by Architect agent 2026-04-27 (workflow stage 3). Full design
review in `FEAT056_design-review.md` (workflow stage 4).*

### Open-question resolutions

| Q | Decision |
|---|---|
| 1 — Insertion point in chat.tsx | Insert v4 hook **between line 419 and line 422** in `processPhrase` — after triage handling (canHandle, needsClarification short-circuits already returned), before legacy intent classification. |
| 2 — Degraded-result UX | **Silent fallback** (PM proposal). When `dispatchSkill` returns degraded result, log it and fall through to legacy. User never sees the "I couldn't complete that" message — they get the legacy reply instead. Cleaner UX. |
| 3 — Multi-turn pending-context | **v4 bypassed when pending-context exists** (PM proposal). The hook checks `s._pendingContext` and skips v4 if set. Pending-context multi-turn integration is a follow-up. |
| 4 — Badge UX | **Small text under bubble** (e.g., "via priority_planning"). Matches existing minimalist chat style. |
| 5 — Badge tap action | **`Alert.alert`** showing skill id + confidence + routing method + "this didn't help" button. Simple, no new modal infrastructure needed. |
| 6 — `general_assistant` token budget | **3000 tokens.** Sufficient for freeform replies; cost stays low (Haiku). |
| 7 — `general_assistant` data categories | `["objectives", "userProfile", "recentTasks"]` — minimum context for "what am I working on" type freeform questions. No sensitive data. |
| Bonus — Sonnet vs Haiku for general_assistant | **Haiku.** Freeform doesn't need reasoning; cost matters more than quality floor here. |

### Data Models

```ts
// src/types/index.ts — extension to ChatMessage

export interface ChatMessage {
  // ...existing fields...
  /** Set when a v4 skill (not legacy) handled this message. Renders as
   *  a small badge under the assistant bubble. */
  v4Meta?: {
    skillId: string;
    confidence: number;
    routingMethod: "structural" | "direct" | "embedding" | "haiku" | "fallback";
  };
}
```

### Routing decision (extracted as pure function for testability)

```ts
// src/modules/v4Gate.ts (NEW small module)

import type { AppState } from "../types";
import { getV4SkillsEnabled } from "./router";

export interface V4GateInput {
  state: AppState;
  triageLegacyIntent: string | null;
}

/**
 * Should the v4 path attempt to handle this turn?
 *
 * Returns false (skip v4, use legacy) when:
 *   - v4-enabled set is empty (rollback / disabled)
 *   - pending-context multi-turn is in flight
 *   - triage already locked an intent (fast path)
 *
 * Pure function — no I/O. Test seam for the chat.tsx integration.
 */
export function shouldTryV4(input: V4GateInput): boolean {
  if (getV4SkillsEnabled().size === 0) return false;
  if (input.state._pendingContext) return false;
  if (input.triageLegacyIntent) return false;
  return true;
}
```

### chat.tsx integration (sketch — Coder fills in exactly)

```tsx
// Inserted between line 419 and line 422:

if (shouldTryV4({ state: s, triageLegacyIntent: triage.legacyIntent ?? null })) {
  try {
    const routeResult = await routeToSkill({ phrase });
    const dispatchResult = await dispatchSkill(routeResult, phrase, { state: s });
    if (dispatchResult && !dispatchResult.degraded) {
      setMessages((m: Message[]) => [...m, {
        role: "assistant",
        content: dispatchResult.userMessage,
        timestamp: now,
        isQuestion: dispatchResult.clarificationRequired,
        v4Meta: {
          skillId: dispatchResult.skillId,
          confidence: routeResult.confidence,
          routingMethod: routeResult.routingMethod,
        },
      }]);
      setLoading(false);
      return; // v4 handled the turn — skip legacy
    }
    // dispatchResult is null or degraded → fall through to legacy
    if (dispatchResult?.degraded) {
      console.warn("[chat] v4 degraded, falling through to legacy:", dispatchResult.degraded.reason);
    }
  } catch (err: any) {
    // Per design review §6 condition 3: dispatcher should never throw.
    // Defensive: if it does, fall through to legacy and log loud.
    console.error("[chat] v4 hook threw — falling through to legacy:", err);
  }
}

// ...existing line 422 onward unchanged...
let intent = triage.legacyIntent
  ? { type: triage.legacyIntent, tokenBudget: 3000, phrase }
  : await classifyIntentWithFallback(phrase, s);
```

### Badge rendering

The chat-bubble component (need to locate exact file in stage 5; likely a
component referenced from chat.tsx or directly inline) gets a small
addition: when `message.v4Meta` is present, render a one-line label
beneath the bubble's content. Tap handler calls `Alert.alert` with the
metadata. No new component file.

### `general_assistant` skill folder spec

```
src/skills/general_assistant/
├── manifest.json
├── prompt.md
├── context.ts
└── handlers.ts
```

**manifest.json:**

```jsonc
{
  "id": "general_assistant",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Freeform conversational assistant. Handles phrases that don't match a specialized skill (chitchat, opinion questions, meta questions about the assistant itself). Does NOT pretend to perform specialized actions — if the user asks for something a real skill should handle, this skill says so.",
  "triggerPhrases": [
    "tell me a joke",
    "what do you think",
    "tell me about yourself",
    "how are you",
    "what can you do",
    "explain",
    "what's the difference between"
  ],
  "structuralTriggers": [],
  "model": "haiku",
  "dataSchemas": {
    "read": ["objectives", "userProfile", "recentTasks"],
    "write": []
  },
  "supportsAttachments": false,
  "tools": ["submit_general_response"],
  "autoEvaluate": true,
  "tokenBudget": 3000,
  "promptLockedZones": [],
  "surface": null
}
```

**prompt.md** (~150 words): explicitly freeform; mentions the user's
objectives only when relevant; politely redirects to specialized
skills if the user is clearly asking for a specialized action ("Sounds
like you want to plan your day — try asking 'what should I focus on'").

**context.ts:** `{ userProfile: true, objectives: true, recentTasks: { limit: 5 } }`

**handlers.ts:** one tool `submit_general_response(args, ctx)` that
returns `{ success: true, userMessage: args.reply }`.

### Service Dependencies

| Internal | Used for |
|---|---|
| `src/modules/router.ts` (FEAT051) | `routeToSkill`, `getV4SkillsEnabled` |
| `src/modules/skillDispatcher.ts` (FEAT055) | `dispatchSkill` |
| `src/modules/skillRegistry.ts` (FEAT054) | (transitively, via dispatchSkill) |
| `app/_layout.tsx` | `setV4SkillsEnabled(["priority_planning", "general_assistant"])` |

No third-party deps added.

### Design Patterns

- **Pure-function gate (`shouldTryV4`)** keeps the chat.tsx change small
  and the gate logic unit-testable in isolation. Bullet 1 of the
  testability standard.
- **Silent fallback on degraded result.** The dispatcher's `degraded`
  user message is for log/audit, not user UX. Chat surface treats
  degraded the same as null: fall through to legacy.
- **v4 hook is wrapped in try/catch** as a defensive belt-and-suspenders
  against the dispatcher's "never throws" promise. Design review §5
  risk mitigation.
- **Badge data lives in the message object**, not in side state. Renders
  consistently when chat history is reloaded from disk.

### New vs. Reusable Components

**New:**
- `src/skills/general_assistant/` (4 files)
- `src/modules/v4Gate.ts` (pure-function gate)
- `src/modules/v4Gate.test.ts`

**Touched (small additions):**
- `src/types/index.ts` — add `v4Meta?` field to `ChatMessage`
- `app/_layout.tsx` — extend `setV4SkillsEnabled([...])` array to include `general_assistant`
- `app/(tabs)/chat.tsx` — insert v4 hook block (~25 lines) + import statements
- (Bubble renderer file — TBD by Coder when locating in stage 5) — render badge when `v4Meta` present

### Risks & Concerns

- **chat.tsx is complex** — 1000+ lines, multi-turn state, fast paths, multiple setMessages call sites. The hook insertion is surgical (one block) but a typo could break legacy flow. Mitigation: pure-function gate isolates the decision; integration tested via end-to-end scenario in stage 7.
- **Badge component location** — until I locate the bubble renderer it's a known unknown. If it's a heavily-shared component the badge addition could touch more code than expected. Mitigation: if too disruptive, defer badge to FEAT057+ and ship just the wiring + skill in FEAT056.
- **`general_assistant` could route too eagerly.** Its triggerPhrases ("tell me about yourself") might score higher than a specialized skill for borderline phrases. Mitigation: high confidence threshold (0.80) means only clear matches win; ambiguous phrases hit the Haiku tiebreaker which can disambiguate.
- **Fallback path now ALWAYS resolves.** Before FEAT056, an unknown phrase hit `general` intent → vague legacy response. After FEAT056, an unknown phrase hits `general_assistant` skill → real freeform Haiku reply. This is a behavioral improvement, but it does change what the user sees for some phrases. Document as expected.
- **Pending-context multi-turn cannot use v4.** A user mid-clarification can't have v4 handle the follow-up. For v2.02 this is acceptable since `priority_planning` doesn't trigger clarification flows often. Revisit when companion or other clarification-heavy skills ship.

### UX Review Notes

UX is small but real:
1. `general_assistant` replies will feel different from today's "general" intent (more conversational, more direct). Users may prefer it.
2. Skill badge under bubble is new visual. Should match existing label styling (smaller font, muted color).
3. No new screens; no layout changes.

### Testing Notes

#### Unit Tests Required
- `shouldTryV4`: 5 cases (empty enabled set, pending context, legacy intent set, all-clear, all combinations).
- `general_assistant` handlers' `submit_general_response`: returns expected user message.

#### Component Tests Required
- `general_assistant` skill folder validates against the FEAT054 loader.
- The chat surface (or a focused test harness) calls the v4 hook with a stub registry/dispatcher → asserts the right message shape.

#### Integration Tests Required
- End-to-end via `dispatchSkill`: phrase routes to `general_assistant` → freeform reply produced. (Stub LLM with canned response.)
- End-to-end via `dispatchSkill`: phrase routes to `priority_planning` → ranking produced. (Already covered by FEAT055; smoke check that nothing broke.)

#### Regression Tests Required
- Full `npm test` passes (current 265 baseline → 265+ new).
- `npm run build:web` exports successfully.

#### Manual Smoke Required
- Start app with v2.02, type "what should I focus on?" — see ranked output.
- Type "tell me a joke" — see freeform reply.
- Type "add a task" — see same legacy task creation behavior as today (not migrated yet).
- Set `setV4SkillsEnabled([])`, restart, repeat all three — all hit legacy.

#### Scope Isolation Tests Required
**No** — privacy filter ships in Phase 3.

#### Agent Fixtures Required
**No** — chat tests use stub LLM.
